channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Tier 1 geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 Trump Discusses Taiwan Arms Sales With Xi Jinping
item.description: President Trump revealed he discussed US arms sales to Taiwan during his Beijing summit with Xi Jinping, saying a decision would come soon. The issue remains a major flashpoint in US-China relations.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Says Patience With Iran Running Thin
item.description: President Trump stated he would not be "much more patient" with Iran as negotiations continue, while suggesting the quest to retrieve enriched uranium was more about perception than security.

item.title: 🇨🇳🛢️ Trump Discussed Lifting Sanctions on Chinese Companies Buying Iranian Oil
item.description: President Trump revealed he discussed potentially lifting sanctions on Chinese companies purchasing Iranian oil during his Beijing visit, with a decision expected soon.

item.title: 🌍 BRICS Summit Ends Without Joint Statement Over Iran Divisions
item.description: BRICS talks concluded without a joint communique as member states remained divided over the Iran conflict. Iran's foreign minister had urged members to condemn US and Israeli actions.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚔️ Israel Kills Seven in Lebanon Amid Ongoing Talks
item.description: Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon killed at least seven people on Friday, as direct negotiations between the US and Israel continued for a second day.

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸 CIA Director Ratcliffe Meets Cuban Officials in Havana
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe held high-level talks with Cuban officials including Raúl Castros grandson, discussing intelligence cooperation and security issues amid tensions over US energy blockade.

item.title: 🇮🇳🇦🇪 India and UAE sign defense and energy deals during Modi state visit
item.description: India and the UAE signed a framework for a strategic defense partnership and petroleum supply agreements during PM Modi's visit, with the UAE announcing billion in investments. The deals deepen ties as both nations navigate the Iran conflict's impact on energy markets.

item.title: 🇪🇸🎤 Spain PM backs Eurovision boycott, says silence on Gaza "not an option"
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez defended Spain's boycott of Eurovision over Israel's participation, saying it puts the country "on the right side of history" and warning against "double standards" compared to Russia's exclusion.

item.title: 🇺🇦🛸 Ukrainian drone pilots demonstrate edge during NATO drills on Gotland
item.description: Ukrainian drone pilots showcased advanced tactics during NATO exercises on Sweden's Gotland island, demonstrating lessons from combat experience. Meanwhile, a US House discharge petition forced a vote on nearly billion in new Ukraine aid.

item.title: 🇱🇧💸 Iran war deepens Lebanon's economic crisis with job losses and inflation
item.description: The Iran-Israel conflict has worsened Lebanon's economic crisis, with businesses closing, prices spiking, and job losses mounting. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has sent economic shockwaves across the Middle East.

item.title: 🌍 BRICS talks end without joint statement over Iran war divisions
item.description: A BRICS meeting in India failed to produce a joint statement as members could not agree on language regarding the Iran conflict, exposing diplomatic rifts within the bloc.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran doubts US seriousness about talks amid nuclear deadlock
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi expressed skepticism about US intentions in nuclear negotiations, saying Tehran lacks trust in Washington's commitment to a deal.

item.title: 🇰🇷 Samsung workers vote to strike amid AI boom labor tensions
item.description: Samsung Electronics workers voted to strike as the global AI boom creates tensions over wages and working conditions, highlighting labor disputes in the semiconductor industry.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia strikes Kyiv apartment building, killing 24 civilians
item.description: A Russian missile strike on a residential building in Kyiv killed 24 people, including three children, as attacks on Ukrainian cities intensify.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany arrests Ukrainian national on suspicion of spying for Russia
item.description: German authorities arrested a Ukrainian national suspected of espionage for Russia, amid ongoing concerns about foreign intelligence operations in Europe.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US Admiral says Iran's ability to threaten neighbors "dramatically degraded"
item.description: A top US military commander stated that Iran's conventional military capabilities have been significantly weakened by ongoing operations, reducing its ability to threaten regional neighbors.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran has "no trust" in US, will only negotiate if serious
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Tehran lacks trust in Washington and will only engage in meaningful negotiations if the US demonstrates seriousness.

item.title: 🇵🇱 US cancels deployment of 4,000 troops to Poland
item.description: The Pentagon canceled plans to deploy 4,000 US troops to Poland, a surprise decision that renewed questions about US military commitments in Europe.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan mediation with US in "difficult course," deadlock remains over enriched uranium
item.description: Pakistan's mediation efforts between the US and Iran face challenges as negotiations remain deadlocked over the enriched uranium issue, according to Iran's top diplomat.

item.title: 🌍 UN agencies warn Somalia at risk of renewed famine as hunger crisis deepens
item.description: United Nations agencies warned that Somalia faces the risk of renewed famine as the hunger crisis deepens, with millions requiring urgent humanitarian assistance.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan's Q1 GDP likely rose on firm exports
item.description: Japan's economy is expected to show growth in the first quarter, supported by strong export performance despite global uncertainties.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Gazprom's Astrakhan gas plant halted motor fuel output after drone attack
item.description: Gazprom's Astrakhan gas processing plant stopped motor fuel production following a drone attack on May 13, highlighting ongoing infrastructure vulnerabilities.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iraqi parliament approves partial government led by PM Zaidi
item.description: Iraq's parliament approved a partial government led by Prime Minister Zaidi, as the country navigates regional tensions from the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia, Ukraine exchange 205 prisoners each
item.description: Russia and Ukraine conducted a prisoner exchange, with each side returning 205 prisoners in one of the largest exchanges of the conflict.

item.title: 🇧🇾 Russia discusses potential offensive from Belarus toward Ukraine or NATO
item.description: Ukrainian intelligence indicates Russia is reviewing plans for potential operations from Belarusian territory, either south toward Ukraine or against a NATO state.
